findings.title: Single branch identity rejected valid CI contexts
findings.rootCause: Memory modeled origin and runtime as one value.
findings.releaseImpact: Main/PR validation could fail after a valid merge.

findings.title: Current memory and release state was stale
findings.rootCause: Updater retained the previous objective, branch and commit assumptions.
findings.releaseImpact: Release decisions could use obsolete facts.
findings.fixStatus: fixed; regenerated state remains honestly blocked

findings.title: Evidence conflated tested and recording commits
findings.rootCause: Summary had no evidence commit, parent or clean-tree lineage.
findings.releaseImpact: A later metadata commit could be presented as fully tested.

findings.title: No post-evidence integrity guard
findings.rootCause: No command constrained changes after the tested commit.
findings.releaseImpact: Product changes could invalidate evidence silently.

findings.title: Playwright responsibilities were duplicated
findings.rootCause: The functional/full command included specialist projects while CI also ran them.

findings.title: CI summary did not fail closed by job result
findings.rootCause: Artifact downloads were tolerated and aggregate analysis did not enforce every needed job.
findings.releaseImpact: A failed specialist gate could produce ambiguous reporting.

findings.title: Linux visual baselines are absent
findings.rootCause: Only reviewed Windows snapshots are committed.
findings.regressionTest: npm run test:visual on Linux CI

findings.title: Linux baseline workflow was over-broad and weakly gated
findings.rootCause: Old phrase and limited filters allowed whole-suite generation without review grouping.
findings.releaseImpact: Unreviewed images could be mistaken for approved baselines.

findings.title: Pages repeated CI and could build a different commit
findings.rootCause: Deployment triggered independently and checked out the event default.
findings.releaseImpact: Untested or failed code could be deployed.
findings.regressionTest: npm run test:release and npm run build:pages

findings.title: No local pre-main fail-closed guard
findings.rootCause: Release state, evidence, reviews and visual provenance were not checked together.
findings.releaseImpact: Blocked evidence could be merged accidentally.

findings.title: Direct dependencies used latest selectors
findings.releaseImpact: Fresh installs could take unreviewed versions.
findings.fixStatus: fixed using compatible caret ranges anchored to lockfile truth

findings.title: Transitive npm audit advisories remain
findings.rootCause: Lighthouse CLI and lint dependency trees include vulnerable transitive packages; npm's all-fix path is breaking.
findings.releaseImpact: Development/CI tooling carries residual path-traversal and ReDoS risk.
findings.fixStatus: unresolved upstream dependency risk; no unsafe force upgrade

findings.title: Main protection is not source-controlled
findings.releaseImpact: Source changes alone cannot prevent direct main pushes.
findings.regressionTest: test pull request must remain blocked until required checks pass
